task:
Add implementation plans to Phase 2 (macOS Keychain) tasks. Run these commands:

bd update SM-ckp.3 --description "$(bd show SM-ckp.3 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/backend/darwin_keychain.go (//go:build darwin)
- CGO: #import <Security/Security.h>
- Functions: SecItemAdd, SecItemCopyMatching, SecItemDelete
- Protected item: kSecAttrService="envmoat", kSecAttrAccount="envmoat-luk-protected", with SecAccessControlRef
- Cache item: kSecAttrAccount="envmoat-luk-cache", no SecAccessControl, include timestamp in value
- Value format: JSON {"luk": "<hex>", "ts": "<unix>"}
- kSecAttrAccessibleWhenPasscodeSetThisDeviceOnly
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.1 (backend interface)
- Acceptance: StoreLUK creates both items, GetLUK returns from cache first then protected, DeleteLUK removes both" 2>&1

bd update SM-ckp.5 --description "$(bd show SM-ckp.5 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/backend/darwin_keychain.go
- CGO: SecAccessControlCreateWithFlags(NULL, kSecAccessControlUserPresence, kSecAccessControlAllowPasswordChange, &err)
- Attach SecAccessControlRef to SecItemAdd via kSecAttrAccessControl
- Error handling: errSecUserCanceled (-25300), errSecAuthFailed (-25293)
- Fallback: macOS shows login password prompt automatically when biometry unavailable
- Consider 99designs/keychain for basic SecItemAdd/CopyMatching, custom CGO only for SecAccessControl
- Dependencies: SM-ckp.3 (two-item pattern)
- Acceptance: Touch ID prompt appears on GetLUK, cancel returns error, login password fallback works" 2>&1

bd update SM-ckp.1 --description "$(bd show SM-ckp.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/session/
- Files: session.go (TTL management, cache check)
- Default TTL: 15 minutes, configurable via config.yaml
- Sliding window: on each GetLUK success, update cache timestamp
- Cache check: GetLUK reads cache item, check timestamp, if expired delete cache + prompt via protected
- Cross-terminal: Keychain cache item is shared (fixed account name)
- Dependencies: SM-ckp.3 (two-item pattern)
- Acceptance: session stays unlocked for 15min, resets on access, expiry deletes cache, shared across terminals" 2>&1

bd update SM-ckp.2 --description "$(bd show SM-ckp.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (status.go, logout.go) or add to root.go
- status: check Keychain items exist (SecItemCopyMatching with kSecMatchLimitOne), show TTL remaining, show active profile
- logout: SecItemDelete cache item only, print confirmation to stderr
- Debug hint: "Set ENVMOAT_DEBUG=1 for verbose logging"
- Dependencies: SM-ckp.1 (session), SM-gtv.4 (resolver for active profile)
- Acceptance: status shows TTL + keychain state, logout clears cache, next command prompts for auth" 2>&1

bd update SM-ckp.4 --description "$(bd show SM-ckp.4 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (doctor.go) or add to root.go
- exec.Command("fdesetup", "isactive") -> check exit code
- Warn: "FileVault is disabled. Full-disk encryption is recommended."
- Integrate into setup (warn, don't block) and doctor (report status)
- Dependencies: none
- Acceptance: setup warns if FileVault off, doctor reports FileVault status" 2>&1

echo "Done updating Phase 2 tasks."
